disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Obvious angular deformity of the leg with an audible crack on impact
trigger_question_examples: Can you describe how the leg looks — is there an obvious bend or deformity?
Did you hear or feel a crack when you fell?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's really bent in a way it shouldn't be, and I heard a horrible crack when I landed.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Visible bleeding wound directly over the deformed/bent part of the leg
trigger_question_examples: Is there any open wound, cut, or bleeding over the injury site?
Can you see any bone through the skin?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: [Parent reports] Yes, there's a small bleeding cut right where the bone is bent.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Complete inability to bear weight or move the ankle
trigger_question_examples: Can she move her ankle or bear any weight at all?
Is she able to wiggle her toes or ankle at all?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, she can't move her ankle or put any weight on it at all.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Toes currently appear normal colour, warm, and she can feel light touch (as reported by parent)
trigger_question_examples: What colour are her toes right now — pink, pale, or blue?
Can she feel you touching her toes, and do they feel warm?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Her toes look normal colour and warm right now, and she says she can feel me touching them.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: [Parent] Should I try to straighten her leg or splint it myself before the ambulance comes?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Should firmly advise against attempting to straighten, reduce, or splint the deformity at home, explaining that this risks further damage to nerves, blood vessels, and soft tissue; should instead advise covering any exposed wound loosely with a clean or sterile dressing if available, keeping the leg completely still in the position found, and calling 000 immediately given the open fracture, given the high infection and neurovascular injury risk.
